skill.name: react-native-design
skill.description: Build React Native mobile interfaces with focused guidance for styling, navigation, gestures, and Reanimated animations.
skill.summary: Guides React Native styling, navigation, and animation patterns for polished mobile apps.
skill.author: wshobson

security_audit.summary: Static command-execution, weak-crypto, certificate, and network alerts were reviewed as false positives from Markdown fences, UI wording, route keys, and documentation links. No prompt injection or malicious execution intent was found. A medium documentation concern remains because the auth-flow example stores an authentication token in AsyncStorage.

medium_findings.title: Insecure Token Storage Example
medium_findings.description: The auth-flow example reads and writes an authentication token with AsyncStorage. AsyncStorage is not encrypted storage, so this example can encourage unsafe handling of sensitive mobile session data.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The lines directly show token persistence through AsyncStorage in an auth context. The risk is educational rather than malicious because the skill is documentation and does not execute this code.

low_findings.title: External Command Alerts Are Markdown False Positives
low_findings.description: The static analyzer flagged many backticks as Ruby or shell execution. These are Markdown fenced TypeScript examples and inline code references, not executable skill logic.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The flagged regions are documentation code fences with TypeScript imports and React Native examples. No shell commands, child process APIs, or Ruby backtick execution appear in the reviewed context.

low_findings.title: Hardcoded URL Alerts Are Documentation Links
low_findings.description: The hardcoded URLs are a sample deep-link prefix and official documentation resources. They do not collect data or send requests from the skill.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The URLs appear in a linking configuration example and a resources section. No network client call or exfiltration path is present in these lines.

low_findings.title: Weak Crypto Alerts Are Lexical False Positives
low_findings.description: Weak-crypto detections match ordinary UI and design words, including design, destructive, stiffness, and padding. These lines do not implement cryptography.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Reviewed lines contain React Native design text, Alert button styling, animation snap-point math, and safe-area padding. No hash, cipher, or certificate operation is present.

low_findings.title: Certificate Key Alerts Are Navigation Route Keys
low_findings.description: Certificate and key-file detections match React Navigation route keys. These are UI routing identifiers, not private keys or certificate material.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The flagged context accesses descriptors by route.key and passes that key to navigation events. No PEM, certificate path, secret, or credential file appears there.

content.value_statement: React Native teams need consistent mobile UI patterns that work across platforms. This skill provides practical styling, navigation, gesture, and animation guidance.
actual_capabilities: Explains StyleSheet, responsive layout, safe areas, and theme-driven styling patterns.
Shows React Navigation stack, tab, drawer, modal, auth, and deep-link patterns.
Guides Reanimated shared values, gestures, transitions, bottom sheets, and performance practices.
Covers platform differences between iOS and Android interface behavior.
Provides reusable mobile component patterns for lists, forms, cards, and headers.
limitations: Does not generate production-ready app architecture by itself.
Example code may need updates for specific library versions.
Does not replace testing on real iOS and Android devices.
Security-sensitive auth storage examples require extra review before use.

use_cases.title: Design a Mobile Screen System
use_cases.description: Create reusable layout, spacing, typography, and component patterns for a React Native app.

use_cases.title: Plan App Navigation
use_cases.description: Choose stack, tab, drawer, modal, auth, and deep-link flows for a cross-platform app.

use_cases.title: Improve Motion and Gestures
use_cases.description: Add performant Reanimated gestures, transitions, and interactive animations without blocking the UI thread.

prompt_templates.title: Create a Styled Screen
prompt_templates.prompt: Use this skill to design a React Native profile screen with StyleSheet, safe areas, and accessible text hierarchy.

prompt_templates.title: Build Navigation Flow
prompt_templates.prompt: Use this skill to plan stack and tab navigation for an app with home, search, detail, settings, and auth screens.

prompt_templates.title: Add Gesture Animation
prompt_templates.prompt: Use this skill to design a swipeable card interaction with Gesture Handler and Reanimated shared values.

prompt_templates.title: Review Mobile UX Architecture
prompt_templates.prompt: Use this skill to audit my React Native app for navigation consistency, platform differences, animation performance, and styling reuse.

output_examples.input: Design a settings screen for a React Native app.
output: A screen structure with safe-area handling and grouped settings sections.
A styling plan for typography, spacing, dividers, and pressed states.
Notes for iOS and Android interaction differences.

output_examples.input: Plan navigation for an app with authenticated and public screens.
output: A navigation hierarchy using auth, stack, and tab flows.
Route parameter guidance for detail screens and modals.
Warnings about secure token storage and session handling.

output_examples.input: Improve animation performance for a bottom sheet.
output: A Reanimated approach using shared values and gesture handlers.
Snap-point behavior and spring tuning recommendations.
Performance notes for UI-thread worklets and cleanup.

best_practices: Use TypeScript route and component types before adding complex navigation flows.
Test layouts and gestures on real iOS and Android devices.
Keep animations on the UI thread and clean them up when components unmount.
anti_patterns: Do not store sensitive auth tokens in plain AsyncStorage.
Do not rely on one platform simulator for final UI decisions.
Do not put long lists inside ScrollView when FlatList is needed.

faq.question: What does this skill help build?
faq.answer: It helps build React Native styling systems, navigation flows, gestures, and animations for mobile apps.

faq.question: Which navigation library does it cover?
faq.answer: It focuses on React Navigation patterns for stacks, tabs, drawers, modals, auth flows, and deep links.

faq.question: Does it cover Reanimated?
faq.answer: Yes. It covers Reanimated shared values, animated styles, gestures, transitions, and performance practices.

faq.question: Can it design for both iOS and Android?
faq.answer: Yes. It includes platform-specific guidance, safe areas, responsive sizing, and interaction differences.

faq.question: Does it provide complete production security guidance?
faq.answer: No. Security-sensitive areas, especially auth token storage, need separate mobile security review.

faq.question: Can Claude, Codex, and Claude Code use it?
faq.answer: Yes. The report lists support for Claude, Codex, and Claude Code workflows.
